exports.content: In Ma et al.'s continuum description of twisted few-layer graphite, the numerical twist angle at which the two primary moire bands become nearly flat is about 1.05 degrees; this equals the principal magic-angle value obtained for twisted bilayer graphene within the same continuum-model formalism, while the claim is scoped to the primary two-band pair rather than all moire bands [@Ma2020].
